title: RDBMS Market Overview — Computer Associates Sales Training
author: Peter S. Kastner
abstract: A 31-slide competitive intelligence training deck prepared by Aberdeen Group analyst Peter S. Kastner for Computer Associates' sales force. Covers 1997–1998 RDBMS strategies and competitive positioning for IBM DB2, Informix, Oracle, Sybase, Microsoft SQL Server, and CA-Ingres. Includes Aberdeen Group's vendor rating framework across scalability, distributed technology, open technology, development tools, and supplier solutions. Contains dedicated weakness assessments for each vendor, hardware platform revenue share data, and strategic guidance for selling CA-Ingres against incumbent rivals.
importance_rationale: Primary-source sales-enablement material produced by a leading analyst firm at a pivotal moment in RDBMS consolidation; captures practitioner-level competitive intelligence rarely preserved in public archives.
relevance_rationale: Directly documents the competitive landscape for all major 1997 RDBMS vendors including CA-Ingres, with Aberdeen Group ratings and weakness profiles useful for historical RDBMS-market research.
prescience_rationale: Accurately anticipates Universal Server extensions adding longevity to SQL, Java's rise as a client-server language, NT moving into enterprise tiers, and object-relational convergence; also foreshadows Informix's financial difficulties and Sybase's decline.
